text:
Once upon a time, there was a cheerful little bird named Benny who lived in a sprawling oak forest. Benny was loved by all the forest creatures, from the shy squirrel gossipers to the boisterous babbling brooks. Though Benny was not particularly talented in bird songs, her enthusiasm was unmatched.

One bright morning, as the dewdrops glistened on the forest leaves, Benny decided she wanted to organize a big festival. She fluttered her wings energetically, fluttering past leaves and flowers to gather treats to decorate the event. She had made splendid feathered hats and golden sunflowers.

Armed with homemade pastries and birdie brooms, Benny flew from one corner of the forest to another. Soon, all the animals lined up to attend. There were squeaky-voiced parrots teaching harmony, nimble squirrels demonstrating acrobatic bird tricks, and even wise old owls giving tips on forest wisdom.
